head.hi: The Secretary of State to the Embassy in Yugoslavia

div.p: 495. Dudel 45. Reference your Niact Deldu 71 Aug 14, full powers went forward you sometime ago as general formality. In view decision not to sign convention these should now be disregarded.

Dept believes final act should not be signed even if no miscellaneous resolutions, protocols, and Soviet convention attached. Dept may wish to denounce whole proceedings later, therefore would prefer no signature of final act.

Dept assumes tel from Paris sent Dept as 4216; and rptd Belgrade as 104 Aug 14,Not printed. Ambassador Caffery reported from Paris in this telegram that the French Foreign Ministry had stated that it believed that the point of view of the United States, rather than that of the British, was correct and that it would so inform the French delegation in Belgrade. (840.811/8–1448) supersedes views expressed Dept by French Embassy Aug 14 reported to you by niact 494 Dudel 44.
